top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic Hong Kong Cyclothon's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before November.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Hong Kong Cyclothon's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 31 miles.
31 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
2-minute all-out efforts on mixed terrain. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Hong Kong Cyclothon's mixed terrain actually does to you.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Hong Kong Cyclothon, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Hong Kong Cyclothon's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Hong Kong Cyclothon. Train it until it feels boring.
5-second power closes gaps and wins field sprints. At Hong Kong Cyclothon, neuromuscular snap is a tactical tool, not just a finish-line move.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
Base fitness for Hong Kong Cyclothon. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.

race_overlay.nutrition: Standard hydration and fueling. One bottle per hour minimum. Gel or bar every 45 minutes at race intensity.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, VO2max, and Gravel Specific to prepare you for 31 miles of mixed terrain in Hong Kong, China.
